steps: Read the scatterplot: data starts near $y=90$ at $x=0$ and falls to near $y=0$ at $x \approx 9$. That's a drop of ~90 over a run of ~9, so slope $\approx -10$. This eliminates choices B and C (slope $-0.1$).
Between A ($y=80-10x$) and D ($y=90-10x$): the y-intercept at $x=0$ reads ~$90$, not $80$.
Confirm with $x=4$: the graph shows $y \approx 50$. Choice D gives $90-40=50$ ✓; Choice A gives $80-40=40$ ✗.
choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '$y=80-10x$': y-intercept $80$ is too low; at $x=4$ predicts $y=40$ but graph shows $\approx 50$. ✗
  • Choice B — '$y=80-0.1x$': Slope $-0.1$ is far too shallow for data that drops ~90 units over 9 units of $x$. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y=90-0.1x$': Correct y-intercept but slope $-0.1$ is far too shallow. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y=90-10x$': y-intercept $90$ ✓, slope $-10$ ✓, passes through $(4,50)$ ✓. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
